qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Attacker-controlled HTML string input triggers DOM XSS during PDF rendering
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable text-source path and same root cause as the advisory: `html2pdf().from(src, 'string')` feeds attacker-controlled HTML into `innerHTML`, strips only literal `<script>` tags, and later appends the resulting DOM into `document.body`; the advisory fix replaces that incomplete sanitization with DOMPurify.

selected_finding.title: Attacker-controlled HTML string input triggers DOM XSS during PDF rendering

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory's exact vulnerable code path for HTML string sources, and the copied PoC achieved end-to-end JavaScript execution in the vulnerable page origin during rendering.
evidence: The advisory states that html2pdf.js is vulnerable when given a text source rather than an element, and fix commit `988826e336035b39a8608182d7b73c0e3cd78c7b` replaces ad hoc `<script>` stripping in `src/utils.js` with `DOMPurify.sanitize(opt.innerHTML)`.

The shipped reproduction bundle preserves the same vulnerable flow: `createElement()` assigns attacker-controlled `innerHTML`, removes only `<script>` tags, and `toContainer()` appends the resulting DOM into `document.body`.

A fresh run from `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_549ae84c/.judge/poc_runs/html_string_dom_xss/run.log` printed `{"triggered":true,"status":"triggered","marker":"yes"}`, which shows the inline `onerror` payload executed in-origin and changed live DOM state.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied browser-based PoC completed successfully and printed `{"triggered":true,"status":"triggered","marker":"yes"}` after loading the page, proving that attacker-controlled HTML supplied through the string source executed JavaScript and modified the live DOM in the vulnerable origin.
